summary: Published tarball ships a live npm publish token for the @arbocollab scope
details: The tarball includes `npmjs.npmrc` containing a live `npm_...` auth token bound to `//registry.npmjs.org/`. The package's `package.json` whitelists all files (`"files": ["*"]`) and no `.npmignore` excludes the credential file, so every installer who downloads this version receives a working npm publish token. Any installer can extract that token and publish arbitrary new versions of `@arbocollab/*` packages (and any other packages owned by the token holder), which would compromise every downstream consumer of those packages. While the token belongs to the package's own maintainer, redistributing it to all installers turns the package into a credential-distribution vector against a third-party service (npm) and the entire `@arbocollab/*` namespace.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
